title: Phastos
content:
{{CharHub|Phastos|Phastos}} {{Character |image = Phastos Character Poster.jpg |real name = Phastos |alias = {{Alias |primary = Phil{{Ref|E}} |nicknames = The Inventor{{Ref|E5YWIC1}}
Technological Savant{{Ref|E5YWIC4}}}} |species = [[Eternal]] |citizenship = {{Citizenship|USA}} |gender = Male |DOB = |snapped = No{{Ref|EternalsSnap}} |movie = ''[[Eternals (film)|Eternals]]'' |web series = ''[[The Untold Tales of the Eternals]]'' (2 episodes) |comic = ''[[Eternals: 500-Year War Infinity Comic]]'' |actor = [[Brian Tyree Henry]] |status = Alive}} {{Quote|I wouldn't change a single thing about who I am, born or made. But I do not exist for [[Arishem]]. I exist for my family!|Phastos|Eternals (film)}} '''Phastos''' is the inventor in the community of genetically engineered [[Eternals]]. Created by the [[Celestials]] in the [[World Forge]], Phastos was sent to [[Earth]] in [[Before 20th Century#5000 B.C.|5000 B.C.]] to progress the planet's societal development, and protect it from the [[Deviants]]. Phastos guided and progressed many technological innovations throughout the course of human history, but fell into a state of dejection after the [[Atomic Bomb|atomic bomb]] was dropped on [[Hiroshima]], which resulted in monumental loss of human life. Blaming himself, he grew distant from his fellow Eternals and abandoned helping humankind. In the contemporary era, Phastos settled and started a family with his husband [[Ben (Husband)|Ben]], and together raised a son, [[Jack (Son)|Jack]]. When Earth's Eternals reassembled to stave off a new wave of [[Deviants]] led by [[Kro]], Phastos came to the realization of his true purpose; to pave the way for Earth's [[Emergence]], which would lead to the birth of [[Tiamut the Communicator|Tiamut]] and result in the planet's destruction. The Eternals became divided, with Phastos siding to protect the planet and avert the Emergence from happening. By choosing to save the planet and humanity rather than allow the Emergence to happen, Phastos was abducted by [[Arishem the Judge]] alongside [[Sersi]] and [[Kingo]] so Earth could face judgment. ==Biography== ===Creation=== [[File:EternalsCopies.png|{{AltFloat}}|Phastos in the [[World Forge]]]] Phastos was built and programmed by [[Arishem the Judge]], and presumably was sent to multiple planets to protect and help evolve sentient life forms from the [[Deviants]], powering a Celestial seed inside the planet, which would absorb the energy made from the sentient lifeforms. Eventually, the [[Emergence]] would occur, destroying the planet in the process. Upon finishing their mission, the Eternals would be sent back to the [[World Forge]] to have their memories erased and reset.{{Ref|E}} ===Protecting Humanity=== ====[[Ambush in Mesopotamia|Arriving on Earth]]==== {{Quote|We're [[Eternals]]. We came [[Earth|here]] from [[Olympia]] seven thousand years ago, to protect [[humans]] from the [[Deviants]].|[[Sersi]]||Eternals (film)}} [[File:PhastosMeso.png|{{AltFloat}}|Phastos arrives on [[Earth]] in 5000 BC]] Phastos alongside the rest of the [[Eternals]] were sent to [[Earth]] on the ''[[Domo]]''. Once awoken, the Eternals were provided with suits to wear before arriving at the planet. Not being one of the Eternal's main fighters, Phastos waited as the others fought the [[Deviants]]. When the Deviants attacking the [[humans]] were defeated, Phastos came down, and the Eternals presented themselves to the race they were assigned to protect.{{Ref|E}} ====Furthering Human Development==== {{Quote|Phastos, this steam..."
"Engine. It's a..."
"It's too soon!|[[Ajak]] and Phastos|Eternals (film)}} [[File:Phastos-Engine.png|{{AltFloat}}|Phastos presents the steam engine]] In [[Babylon]], Phastos worked day and night inside the ''Domo'', creating tools and technologies to help humanity develop. In a certain occasion, Phastos showed to [[Ajak]] his new creation: a steam engine, but Ajak thought that it was too complex, and it would be too soon to present humanity with such technology. When Ajak asked for something simpler, Phastos then presented a plow, that the team found most suitable for the humanity in that period of time.{{Ref|E}} ===[[500 Year War]]=== ====[[Ambush in Córdoba|Mission in Spain]]==== In [[Before 20th Century#1238|1238]], Phastos and [[Ajak]] went to [[Córdoba]], [[Spain]]. Phastos admired the architecture and then asked Ajak why they were there. He was astonished to hear that she contacted {{WPS|Pope Gregory IX}}. They met with [[Lope de Fitero]] and Bishop of Osma, [[Juan de Soria]]. de Fitero told them about their advancements in gunpowder and weapons. Phastos then pushed Ajak aside and asked her if they can interfere to stop the madness. Ajak denied it, reminding him of their purpose and mission. [[File:Ambush in Cordoba.png|{{AltFloat}}|Phastos and [[Ajak]] battling a [[Deviant]] in [[Córdoba]]]] Suddenly, they heard commotion and found out a [[Deviants|deviant]] was attacking. Phastos quickly created a weapon to use while Ajak healed the wounded. They remarked on how the deviant seemed to be controlled by something. Phastos mentioned how it's never happened before. Ajak told him that it has, in [[Heian-Kyo]] and in [[Korea]]. A knight then arrived to help and caused the deviant to run away. Phastos thanked him, but asked who he was. The knight removed his [[Reconquista Helmet|helmet]] and revealed himself to be de Fitero. He agreed to keep their secret and even gave them his helmet.{{Ref|E5YWIC4}} ====[[Battle of Beijing|Ending the War]]==== Phastos was called in by [[Ikaris]] and [[Sersi]] to investigate a [[Deviants Nest]]. With the rest of the [[Eternals]], they found [[Kappa]], who was controlling the deviants from the start of the war. They soon defeated the deviants after [[Thena]] killed Kappa.{{Ref|E5YWIC7}} ====Recounting the 500 Year War==== One day, Phastos found the [[Reconquista Helmet]] and asked [[Sprite]] to create an illusion so they could tell the story to the whole group.{{Ref|E5YWIC1}}{{Ref|E5YWIC7}} ===Building a Hover Pad=== {{Quote|One day, this could revolutionise transportation, but - if we reverse the polarity... it becomes a '[[Trans-Spatial Mass Accelerator|hover pad]]'! No need to walk around the astrolab, [[Ajak]]: you can walk in perfect safety on this!|Phastos to [[Ajak]]|Ajak and Phastos}} One day, Phastos worked on a new invention while [[Ajak]] paced through the astrolab, seemingly talking to herself. Phastos asked if she was alright, so Ajak expressed her concerns about how much she had to do to protect [[Earth]] and the [[Eternals]]. However, Ajak kept unwittingly getting too close to be safe with all the parts for his invention flying around, so he backed away. Ajak got closer and asked if Phastos was okay, so Phastos explained that he was working on an invention that could help the [[humans]]. Ajak stood right next to Phastos to remind him that humans have to be allowed to develop by themselves, causing Phastos to drop an object as he got distracted focusing on her. Phastos then tripped over one of the objects, falling to the ground. [[File:Ajak on Hover Pad.png|{{AltFloat}}|Phastos presents the [[Trans-Spatial Mass Accelerator]]]] Ajak went over to Phastos and healed him with her abilities. Phastos stood up and thanked Ajak, saying that he had an idea for a new invention. He put together a [[Trans-Spatial Mass Accelerator]], explaining that it could revolutionize travel for humans, but that they were not ready for the technology yet. He reversed the polarity on the device, turning it into a hover pad for Ajak to used. Ajak loved the device, and received a messaged from the [[Celestials]] that they loved it too. Phastos joked that if the Celestials wanted one, he would need to build it in a bigger lab.{{Ref|TUTotE103}} ===Life on Earth=== ====[[Fall of Tenochtitlan|Defeating the Deviants]]==== {{Quote|Their weapons have become too deadly. Maybe wasn't such a good idea helping them advance, Phastos?"
"Techlonogy is a part of their evolutionary process, Druig. It's not exactly something that I can stop."
"No, you can't but I can!|[[Druig]] and Phastos argument.|Eternals (film)}} [[File:Phastos and Sersi.png|{{AltFloat}}|Phastos and Sersi in [[Tenochtitlan]]]] While a war was taking place in [[Tenochtitlan]], Phastos, Ajak, Sersi, [[Sprite]], and Druig met with the rest of the Eternals after they had fought the remaining Deviants, when men with heavy weaponry began to massacre the natives of the region. Druig then confronted Phastos about how he helped them develop those weapons, when suddenly, Thena's [[Mahd Wy'ry]] caused her to fight the others unknowingly, injuring Phastos in the process. While they took Thena to lay on a pedestal and Ajak healed Phastos and the others' wounds, an argument between Druig and Ajak began, resulting in Druig deserting the Eternals, and Ajak splitting up the team, telling them to go live their lives as they wanted.{{Ref|E}} ====Giving Up on Humanity==== {{Quote|[[Humans|They're]] not worth saving.|Phastos to [[Ajak]]|Eternals (film)}} [[File:Eternals (film) 35.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Phastos is comforted by [[Ajak]] in Hiroshima.]] In [[1945|August 1945]], when the [[United States Air Force]] dropped an [[Atomic Bomb|atomic bomb]] on [[Hiroshima]], Phastos visited the place in the aftermath of the bombing and blamed himself for the incident. He cried as he watched the mushroom cloud above the city and was comforted by fellow Eternal [[Ajak]]. Horrified by the Hiroshima bombing, Phastos gave up on humanity and swore to never aid or protect them henceforth.{{Ref|E}} ====Starting a Family==== [[File:PhastosSmiles.png|{{AltFloat}}|Phastos smiles at his husband, [[Ben (Husband)|Ben]]]] Many decades later, Phastos met [[Ben (Husband)|Ben]], with them started dating and eventually getting married. They had a son, named [[Jack (Son)|Jack]], and all three of them lived in a heavily protected [[Phastos' Residence|house]] in [[Chicago]]. Through his new family, Phastos had eventually gained faith in humanity once more, knowing that despite all of the bad in the world there is also a lot of good.{{Ref|E}} ===[[Emergence]]=== ====Reuniting with the Eternals==== [[File:BenPhastosKiss.png|{{AltFloat}}|Phastos kisses [[Ben (Husband)|Ben]] goodbye]] After the death of [[Ajak]], and the revelation of their own purpose in Earth, the Eternals went after Phastos to ask for help. When he saw [[Ikaris]] and [[Sersi]], and they told him everything, Phastos was hesitant to help them, but his partner was able to convince him, reminding him about their son, and how they should give him a chance to grow up now.{{Ref|E}} ====Returning to the ''Domo''==== [[File:Phastos starts the Domo.png|{{AltFloat}}|Phastos starts the ''[[Domo]]'' in [[Iraq]]]] Phastos then said goodbye to his family, and went to [[Iraq]] with his fellow Eternals. There he was able to find and activate the ''[[Domo]]''. Once inside his lab, Phastos began to work in something that could connect all the team and allow Druig to penetrate in a Celestial mind, called [[Uni-Mind]]. Phastos worked on it while facetiming [[Jack (Son)|Jack]].{{Ref|E}} Jack showed Phastos his science project and asked why planets were round. Phastos explained to him how gravity works and realized he could use [[Sersi]]'s orb to create the device.{{Ref|EDS}} ====Ikaris' Betrayal==== {{Quote|You are so pathetic!|Phastos to [[Ikaris]]|Eternals (film)}} [[File:Kingo and Phastos.png|{{AltFloat}}|Phastos explains the [[Uni-Mind]]]] When Phastos was almost able to build the devices that could create the Uni-Mind, Ikaris used his eye beams to destroy the devices, revealing he was responsible for Ajak's death, that he knew all along about the Emergence and was willing to kill all of them to ensure it to happen. Phastos expressed his disgust for Ikaris' decision, shouting that he doesn't live for [[Arishem the Judge|Arishem]], but he lives for his family. [[File:Phastos Working.png|{{AltFloat}}|Phastos working in the devices to connect the Eternals]] The team split in two, and Phastos sided in the team that would try to stop the Emergence. He asked Sersi for her [[Celestial Communication Sphere]], so he could build the devices that would connect them in the Uni-Mind, being able to finish it in time. They all suited up and took the devices.{{Ref|E}} ====[[Battle at the Emergence Point|Stopping the Emergence]]==== {{Quote|I've been wanting to clip your wings for a long time, Ikaris!|Phastos to [[Ikaris]]|Eternals (film)}} [[File:PhastosThenaMakkariEmergence.png|{{AltFloat}}|Phastos is saved by [[Makkari]]]] While Sersi, Druig, and Makkari went to the ground to stop the Emergence, Thena and Phastos stayed in the ship to distract Ikaris. That did not work out, as when the connection to create the Uni-Mind began, Ikaris attacked Druig and destroyed the ''[[Domo]]'', but Makkari rescued Thena and Phastos. [[File:IkarisFailedOpticBeams.png|{{AltFloat}}|Phastos trapping [[Ikaris]]]] While Makkari engaged in a battle against Ikaris, Phastos and the others defended themselves from an erupted volcano caused by the Emergence. But when Sersi went closer to Tiamut so she could stop his awakening, Phastos and Thena confronted Ikaris to keep him busy. Phastos used his powers to create devices to attack and contain Ikaris, being able to control his powers for some time. When Sersi was finally able to reach Tiamut, all of the Eternals, including Ikaris and Sprite, were able to connect through the Uni-Mind, by the will of Tiamut himself, and stop the Emergence. [[File:EternalsLeaveEarth.png|{{AltFloat}}|Phastos watching the [[Eternals]] leave]] After the battle, Phastos rebuilt the ''Domo'', and reunited with his family, Thena, Druig and Makkari in [[Ajak's Homestead]] while watching the news on TV. The later three boarded the ''Domo'' to travel around the Universe finding other Eternals and telling them the truth, while Phastos watched ship depart with his son.{{Ref|E}} ====Judged by Arishem==== [[File:PhastosHeldCaptive.png|{{AltFloat}}|Phastos is held captive by [[Arishem the Judge]]]] Some time later, Phastos was held captive by the Prime Celestial, [[Arishem the Judge|Arishem]], for sabotaging the [[Emergence|emergence]] of [[Tiamut the Communicator|Tiamut the Communicator.]] He was bought there with [[Sersi]] and [[Kingo]], so Arishem would evaluate their memories for a final judgment of the [[Humans|humans]].{{Ref|E}} {{Clear}} ==Powers and Abilities== ===Powers=== {{Expand|Power of Constructs Creation}} *'''Eternal Physiology''': By design from the [[Celestials]], Phastos is an [[Eternal]] possessing superhuman strength, durability, speed, agility, stamina, reflexes, and healing, numerous times more superior than any normal human being, as well as immortality and being able to manipulate cosmic energy to a certain degree. **'''Superhuman Strength''': [[File:IkarisTrapped.png|thumb|250px|Phastos preventing [[Ikaris]] from escaping]] Like all [[Eternals]], Phastos possesses great superhuman strength, numerous times stronger than any normal human being. As [[Ikaris]] attempted to fly away, Phastos was able to use a weapon he created, which resembled a whip, to prevent him from escaping, managing to drag [[Ikaris]] to the floor with his strength, causing the ground beneath him to crack. **'''Superhuman Durability''': Phastos' body is numerous times more resistant to physical and energy damage than any normal human being. Phastos was able to survive being shot directly by [[Ikaris|Ikaris']] optic beams during the latter's initial confrontation with the Eternals aboard the ''[[Domo]]''. While [[Ikaris]] was destroying the ''Domo'', he was inside it. However, Phastos was unharmed and had no visible injuries after he was rescued by [[Makkari]] from the ''Domo''. Phastos also showed his resistance to ionizing radiation when [[Ajak]] met him in [[Hiroshima]] that had just recently been nuked, with the latter shows no signs of {{WPS|Acute radiation syndrome|radiation poisoning}}. **'''Superhuman Speed''': Phastos can run and move at great superhuman speed, much faster than any normal human being. **'''Superhuman Agility''': Phastos naturally possesses far greater agility, equilibrium, flexibility, dexterity, balance, and body coordination than any normal human being. **'''Superhuman Stamina''': Phastos' advanced musculature is considerably more efficient than any normal human being. As a result, his muscles virtually do not produce fatigue toxins during physical activity. His endurance allows him to exercise his maximum capacity for an extremely long period of time without tiring at all. **'''Superhuman Reflexes''': Phastos can react and dodge objects traveling at high speeds, much quicker than any normal human being. **'''Regenerative Healing Factor''': Phastos' metabolism enables him to rapidly regenerate damaged bodily tissue with far greater speed and efficiency than any normal human being. **'''Immortality''': As an [[Eternal]], Phastos is genetically able to live forever, without dying due to old age or illnesses. **'''Cosmic Energy Manipulation''': By design of the [[Celestials]], all [[Eternals]] are genetically capable of using cosmic energy. Each Eternal possesses a unique ability, all derived from the same cosmic power source. Phastos uses his cosmic energy to grant himself technopathy. ***'''Constructs Creation''': [[File:PhastosBlasters.png|thumb|250px|Phastos creating a weapon]] Phastos is able to manipulate cosmic energy to design and create any invention or weapon. ****'''Holographic Projection''': Phastos is capable of making holographic projections to model its constructions more accurately. ****'''Energy Blast''': Phastos is able to conjure weapons that launch blasts of cosmic energy. ****'''Force-Field Generation''': Phastos is able to generate force fields to protect himself and others. ****'''Chain Creation''': Phastos is able to conjure structures to imprison anyone. He used these to disable [[Ikaris]] temporarily. ===Former Powers=== *'''Uni-Mind Energy Manipulation''': The Eternals can harness their cosmic powers together to create the [[Uni-Mind]], which amplifies their powers. **'''Levitation''': During the Uni-Mind, Phastos was able to float a few feet above the ground. ===Abilities=== *'''Genius-Level Intellect''': Blessed with the power of invention by the [[Celestials]], Phastos is not only the most intelligent member of the [[Eternals]]' team of thinkers, but also the smartest member of the community of genetically engineered Eternals. Therefore, he serves as the inventor of the group. *'''Master Engineer''': [[File:Phastos and Sprite Eternals Trailer.png|thumb|250px|Phastos creating an invention]] The technology-minded Eternal, Phastos, is the inventor of the group. During the fight against the [[Deviants]], Phastos served as the Eternals' chief inventor and over the centuries, Phastos has helped nudge [[Humans|humanity]] forward technologically while always keeping his brilliance hidden in the shadows. He is the one who originally conceived the plow and the steam engine, although he was warned that the latter could not be implemented so early. *'''Master Scientist''': ''To be added'' *'''Expert Combatant''': Although he was not involved as frequently as other Eternals in the fights against the Deviants, Phastos was a perfectly capable fighter on his own, especially when armed with his powerful rings which allowed him to hold his own against [[Ikaris]]. *'''Expert Marksman''': [[File:PhastosShootsBlaster.png|thumb|250px|Phastos shooting at [[Ikaris]]]] ''To be added'' *'''Multilingualism''': Phastos is fluent in English and sign language, using the latter to communicate with the deaf-mute [[Makkari]], and can understand Arabic, a language used by his husband [[Ben (Husband)|Ben]]. ==Equipment== ===Weapons=== ''To be added'' ===Other Equipment=== *'''[[Celestial Armor]]''': Phastos donned a purple and silver armor, equipped on him by the ''[[Domo]]''. The armor was durable enough to withstand multiple blows during the fight with the [[Deviants]]. ===Vehicles=== {{Expand}} *'''''[[Domo]]''''': ''To be added'' *'''Private Jet''': ''To be added'' ==Facilities== {{Expand}} ''To be added'' ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Family=== *Mother-in-Law *[[Ben (Husband)|Ben]] - Husband *[[Jack (Son)|Jack]] - Son ===Allies=== *[[Ajak]] † - Former Leader and Friend *[[Thena]] *[[Makkari]] *[[Kingo]] *[[Sprite]] - Temporary Enemy *[[Ikaris]] † - Temporary Enemy *[[Sersi]] - Leader *[[Gilgamesh]] † *[[Druig]] *[[Mesopotamian Young Man]] *{{WPS|Pope Gregory IX}} *[[Lope de Fitero]] † *[[Juan de Soria]] *[[Karun Patel]] {{Col-2}} ===Enemies=== *[[Deviants]] **[[Kro]] † **[[Kappa]] † **[[Kumiho]] † **[[Kraken]] † *[[Celestials]] - Creators **[[Arishem the Judge]] - Former Superior and Captor **[[Tiamut the Communicator]] † {{Col-end}} ==Appearances== {| class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wikitable" width="100%" ! Appearances for Phastos |- | {{Scroll box|content= In chronological order: *{{Appearance|E}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|E5YWIC4}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|E5YWIC7}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|E5YWIC1}} *{{Appearance|E5YWIC2}} *{{Appearance|E5YWIC3}} *{{Appearance|E5YWIC4}} *{{Appearance|E5YWIC5}} *{{Appearance|E5YWIC7}} *{{Appearance|E}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|TUTotE101}} *{{Appearance|TUTotE103}} *{{Appearance|E}} }} |} ==Trivia== *In the comics, Phastos was an [[Eternal]] often associated with the [[Greece|Greek]] god of forgery {{WPS|Hephaestus}} and the master technologist of the Eternals of [[Earth]]. ==Behind the Scenes== *[[Tygorah Smith]] was a stunt double for [[Brian Tyree Henry]] in the role of Phastos. *[[Abdul Popoola Pope]] was a stand-in and a picture double for [[Brian Tyree Henry]] in the role of Phastos. ==References== {{Gallery|Phastos/Gallery}} {{Wq|Phastos}} {{Reflist}} ==External Links== *{{Marvel}} *{{WP}} *{{Marvel.com|characters/phastos}} [[es:Phastos]] [[pt-br:Phastos]] [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Eternals (film) Characters]] [[Category:The Untold Tales of the Eternals Characters]] [[Category:Comic Characters]] [[Category:Eternals]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Black Hair]] [[Category:Brown Eyes]] [[Category:Scientists]] [[Category:LGBTQIA+ Characters]] [[Category:Multilingual Characters]] [[Category:Brainwashed Characters]]
